code:
class Solution:
    def isMatch(self, text: str, pattern: str) -> bool:
        m, n = len(text), len(pattern)

        # dp[i][j] will be true if text[0...i-1] matches pattern[0...j-1]
        dp = [[False] * (n + 1) for _ in range(m + 1)]

        # Base case: empty text matches empty pattern
        dp[0][0] = True

        # Helper function to check if a single character from text matches a single character from pattern
        def char_matches(char_t, char_p):
            return char_p == '.' or char_t == char_p

        # Fill the first row: text is empty, pattern may match if it consists of 'x*y*'-like segments
        for j in range(1, n + 1):
            if pattern[j-1] == '*':
                # '*' matches zero occurrences of the preceding character (pattern[j-2])
                # So pattern[j-1] and pattern[j-2] are effectively skipped
                dp[0][j] = dp[0][j-2]

        # Fill the rest of the DP table
        for i in range(1, m + 1):
            for j in range(1, n + 1):
                if char_matches(text[i-1], pattern[j-1]):
                    # If the last characters match (or pattern character is '.'),
                    # then the match depends on the match of preceding substrings
                    dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1]
                elif pattern[j-1] == '*':
                    # Case 1: '*' matches zero occurrences of the preceding element (pattern[j-2])
                    # In this case, pattern[j-2]* is effectively skipped
                    dp[i][j] = dp[i][j-2]
                    
                    # Case 2: '*' matches one or more occurrences of the preceding element (pattern[j-2])
                    # For this to happen, text[i-1] must match pattern[j-2]
                    if char_matches(text[i-1], pattern[j-2]):
                        # If text[i-1] matches pattern[j-2], then '*' can consume text[i-1]
                        # The problem reduces to matching text[0...i-2] with pattern[0...j-1] (where '*' is still active)
                        dp[i][j] = dp[i][j] or dp[i-1][j]
                # If neither of the above conditions met, dp[i][j] remains False (initialized)
        
        return dp[m][n]
